title: Conchocarpus longifolius
taxonomy: Conchocarpus longifolius is a species of plant in the family Humiriaceae. This family is a part of the order Malpighiales and the clade Rosidae. The order Malpighiales is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the tropics. The clade Rosidae is a large group of flowering plants that also includes other families such as the Fabaceae and the Sapindaceae. Conchocarpus longifolius is native to South America.

intro: Conchocarpus longifolius, also known as the Brazilian angelica tree or angelica of the forest, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, potentially benefiting the treatment of various health conditions, including infections and wounds.
